skill.name: tunit
skill.description: Run TUnit Playwright tests with controlled parallelism, filters, debug guidance, and report options for a .NET test project.
skill.summary: Run TUnit Playwright tests with practical commands for filters, reports, and safer local execution.
skill.author: AndreHogberg

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command hits plus weak cryptography and network reconnaissance indicators. Manual review found the weak cryptography and network reconnaissance indicators are false positives from Markdown text, while the external commands are legitimate dotnet test examples. Risk is medium because the skill directs agents to execute local test projects, which can run repository code and start Playwright resources.

medium_findings.title: External Test Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill gives dotnet run examples for a local TUnit Playwright test project. This is expected for a test-runner skill, but it causes the assistant to execute repository code and may start browsers or a local server.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct shell commands in Markdown and match the skill purpose. Confidence is limited because there is no evidence of malicious command content.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Weak Cryptography and Network Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at lines 3 and 73 and network reconnaissance at line 90. Manual review found no cryptographic operation or network scanning command in those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct line review shows ordinary descriptive text about TUnit, flakiness mitigation, and Playwright PageTest usage. No weak algorithm, scanner, or reconnaissance behavior is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands in Markdown Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run dotnet commands against the local test project. These commands are legitimate but still execute project-controlled code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and executable. The context indicates normal test execution rather than malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: End-to-end test runs can overload local systems or miss useful filters. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise TUnit commands for controlled Playwright execution.
actual_capabilities: Runs the TUnit Playwright suite through dotnet run with the documented project path.
Limits Playwright test execution to one or two parallel tests.
Filters tests by class, exact test name, wildcard pattern, or namespace.
Shows how to produce detailed console output and TRX reports.
Explains project conventions for retries, server readiness, and PageTest-based tests.
limitations: Assumes the .NET test project exists at tests/SummitUI.Tests.Playwright.
Does not install dotnet, TUnit, Playwright browsers, or project dependencies.
Does not analyze failing test output without the user providing results.
Commands may start a local server, browser processes, and repository test code.

use_cases.title: Run the full suite before a release
use_cases.description: A QA engineer can execute all TUnit Playwright tests with reduced parallelism to avoid local resource pressure.

use_cases.title: Debug one component test
use_cases.description: A frontend engineer can filter by class or exact test name when working on a focused UI change.

use_cases.title: Collect test reports for review
use_cases.description: A CI maintainer can request console details or TRX output when comparing local and pipeline results.

prompt_templates.title: Run every test
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full TUnit Playwright test suite and tell me whether it passes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete local test run.

prompt_templates.title: Limit parallel execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the TUnit Playwright tests with one parallel test so the machine stays responsive.
prompt_templates.scenario: Controlled local execution on a constrained workstation.

prompt_templates.title: Filter a focused group
prompt_templates.prompt: Run only the SelectAccessibilityTests TUnit Playwright tests and summarize the failures.
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused validation for one component or test class.

prompt_templates.title: Generate triage output
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the matching keyboard tests with detailed console output and a TRX report for review.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced failure triage that needs structured reporting.

output_examples.input: Run all TUnit Playwright tests with limited parallelism.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs the documented test project with one or two parallel tests, then reports pass status, failures, and relevant timing.

output_examples.input: Run only tests matching a keyboard pattern.
output_examples.output: The assistant applies the TUnit tree node filter for keyboard-named tests and summarizes the selected test results.

output_examples.input: Create a report for the Playwright test run.
output_examples.output: The assistant uses the report option, explains where the report was generated, and highlights any failed tests.

best_practices: Use limited parallelism first when running browser-based tests locally.
Filter by class or exact test name before rerunning an entire suite.
Review test project code before allowing commands from a community skill to execute.
anti_patterns: Do not run the full browser suite repeatedly when a focused filter is enough.
Do not assume dependencies or Playwright browsers are installed without checking.
Do not ignore failed setup or server readiness errors in the test output.

faq.question: What test framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets TUnit tests that use Playwright in a .NET project.

faq.question: Can it run only one test class?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents TUnit tree node filters for class, test name, wildcard, and namespace selection.

faq.question: Does it install Playwright or dotnet dependencies?
faq.answer: No. The skill only provides execution guidance for an existing project setup.

faq.question: Why does the audit mark medium risk?
faq.answer: The skill runs local shell commands. That is expected for testing, but it executes repository-controlled code.

faq.question: Can it create test reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for detailed console output and TRX report generation.

faq.question: Is there evidence of malicious behavior?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed file contains test-running instructions and no prompt injection attempt.
